title: Wrong result when a signal is divided by itself
body:
Hi there!

The IEEE 1800-2017 standard says:

> For the division or modulus operators, if the second operand is a zero, then the entire result value shall be x.

Given that Verilator represents x by zero, I would expect to get 0 every time a division by zero occurs.
However, I noticed that dividing a number by itself gives 1, which is incorrect if my understanding is correct.
An occurrence can be as simple as below, with the assignment `a=0`:

```
module toplevel (
  input logic a,
  output logic y
);
  assign y = a / a;
endmodule
```

By curiosity, I added the flags `-fno-const-bit-op-tree -fno-dfg-peephole -fno-dfg-pre-inline -fno-dfg-post-inline -fno-gate -fno-subst -fno-subst-const` but it did not erase the buggy behavior.

I'm using `Verilator 5.018 2023-10-30 rev v5.004-74-g41e5f6b7d`.

Thanks!
Flavien
